topic: Control System Analysis and Model Predictive Control (MPC)
steps: Define state-space model: mc.ss(A, B, C, D)
Convert to discrete: mc.c2d(sys, sample_time)
Simulate Linear MPC: mc.lmpc(sysd, N, R, T, lambda)
Simulate Quadratic MPC: mc.qmpc(sysd, N, R, T, lambda, Umax, S, I, x0)
Generate plots: bode(sys), nyquist(sys), step(sys)
theory: MataveControl uses Linear (Simplex) or Quadratic (Hildreth's Method) programming for MPC. Hildreth's method is computationally faster in MATLAB. Integral action (I) and slack variables (S) handle steady-state error and output constraints.

topic: MPC with Kalman-Bucy Filter and Mass-Spring-Damper System
steps: Define physical parameters m, k, and b for the system.
Construct the state-space system matrix A.
Initialize MPC parameters including lambda, reference R, and initial state x0.
Simulate MPC using mc.qmpc with quadratic programming.
Plot the control signal results.

params.lambda: Regularization factor for smooth control inputs

theory: MPC implementation for microcontrollers using a Kalman-Bucy filter and integral action added to the reference vector R prior to QP optimization. Models a second-order mass-spring-damper system (m*ddx + b*dx + k*x = F) converted to first-order state-space.

topic: JSON and Binary-JSON (BJData) encoding/decoding via JSONLab
steps: Encode MATLAB data structures into JSON or binary BJData formats
Decode JSON/BJData files into MATLAB variables
Use jdict for creating portable hierarchical data containers
Apply Base64 or Blosc2 encoding for binary data compression and transmission

params.bjdata: Binary JSON format optimized for scientific data
params.base64: Encoding scheme for representing binary data in ASCII string format
params.blosc2: High-performance compressor for binary data within JSON structures
params.encodevarname/decodevarname: Functions to ensure MATLAB variable name compatibility with JSON keys

errors: Parsing mixed-type scalars arrays in bjdata - Fixed in May 2026 update
Base64 newline issues when using zmat - Fixed in Dec 2025 update
theory: JSONLab is a toolbox for converting MATLAB data structures (structs, arrays, cells) into JSON and binary-JSON formats. It enables cross-platform data exchange, supports hierarchical data via jdict, and provides utilities for variable name sanitization and data compression to handle large-scale engineering datasets.

topic: JSONLab toolbox for JSON/Binary-JSON encoding and decoding
steps: Install JSONLab via OS package manager (Fedora, Debian, Ubuntu, Arch) or manual download
Use savejson/loadjson for standard JSON text conversion
Use savebj/loadbj for Binary JData (BJData) processing
Use jdataencode/jdatadecode to transform MATLAB structures into JData-compliant formats
Utilize jsave/jload for portable workspace sharing
Apply {zlib,gzip,base64,lzma,lz4,zstd}encode/decode for in-memory data compression

params.savejson.m: Encodes MATLAB data structures into JSON text
params.loadjson.m: Parses JSON files or strings into MATLAB structures
params.savebj.m: Encodes MATLAB variables into binary BJData format
params.jsave.m/jload.m: Saves/loads entire workspace variables to/from JSON/BJData
params.jdataencode.m: Pre-processes MATLAB data into JData specification format
params.varargin2struct.m: Converts function input arguments to a structure

fixes: Optimized parsers updated for compatibility with MATLAB R2010 and Octave 5.2
theory: Official reference library for JData (V1 Draft-3) and BJData (V1 Draft-2) specifications. Provides portable, robust encoding/decoding of MATLAB structures. Supports JSON-Mmap and multiple compression algorithms including zlib, lz4, and zstd for high-density data storage.

topic: JSONLab v2.9.8 JSON/BJData processing and neuroimaging data handling
steps: Use savejson/loadjson with BuiltinJSON option for native MATLAB encoding/decoding
Employ jsonget/jsonset for memory-mapped reading and writing of JSON/binary files
Query data structures using jsonpath with deep-scan operators
Load .nii, .h5, .snirf, .tsv, and .csv files directly via loadjd.m
Utilize jsoncache and jdlink for on-demand download of dynamic datasets

params.BuiltinJSON: Option to utilize native MATLAB/Octave jsonencode/jsondecode
params.BJData V1 Draft 2: Updated specification setting Little-Endian as default byte order
params.Blosc2: High-performance meta-compressor for large N-D array storage
params.containers.Map: Data type used automatically when keys exceed 63 characters

fixes: Added octavezmat as a fallback for zlib/gzip compression in Octave when ZMat is absent
theory: JSONLab is the official reference library for JData and BJData specifications. It facilitates language-independent data annotation and high-performance serialization, supporting memory-mapping, JSONPath queries, and multi-format parsing for scalable neuroimaging data management.

topic: JSONLab data serialization and JSONPath syntax
steps: Use savejd/loadjd for JSON/BJData I/O.
Access deep struct levels using jsonpath with '..' operator.
Manage remote data caching with jdlink and jsoncache.
Use jsonset/jsonget for key-value manipulation.

params.maxlinklevel: Limit for recursive data link resolution.
params.automap: Auto-switch to map objects for keys > 63 characters.
params.jsonpath: Query syntax supporting deep scans and dot escaping.

errors: error_pos - triggered by non-existent input files.
theory: JSONLab facilitates JSON/BJData/HDF5 serialization in MATLAB/Octave. It supports BJData Draft-2, JSONPath for deep-level structure queries, and automated dictionary/Map conversion for compatibility with long struct field names.

steps: Install Octave via conda on macOS to prevent installation hangs
Use {:} to expand varargin for function arguments
Save JSON with UTF-8 encoding to ensure character compatibility
Use zstdencode/zstddecode for Zstandard compression via ZMat
Link binary jdata files directly inside JSON objects

params.NO_ZMAT: Flag to disable ZMat compression library and use fallbacks
params.EmptyArrayAsNull: Option to toggle encoding of empty arrays as null
params.blosc2: Codec support for high-performance data compression
params.pmat: Portable MAT file format (renamed from jamm)

errors: Octave 6.4+ regexp non-utf8 error - Replace regexp with ismember
containers.Map indentation bug - Fixed for single-element maps
fread issue - Resolved via NO_ZMAT flag logic
fixes: Speed up string encoding and decoding routines
Fix loadjd and savejd file extension matching for jbids
Implement fallback zlib/glib support on Octave using file-based zip/unzip
theory: JSONLab facilitates cross-platform data exchange between MATLAB/Octave and JSON/JData formats. It supports advanced compression (Zstd, Blosc2), portable data hashing (jdatahash), and specific handling for containers.Map, numeric cells, and UTF-8 encoding.
